channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Trees require trimming., 21st April
item.description:
The trees along Hazel Way Lobelys Drive require trimming to stop them interfering with traffic, pedestrians and from damaging properties.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lobleys Drive, Hucclecote, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tyres, 21st April
item.description:
3 tyres dumped outside 46 swallow
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Swallow Crescent, Innsworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Signs littering Street Light posts traffic lights, 21st April
item.description:
Issue: Two Event Notices - Signs attached to street lighting columns and traffic lights not removed after events. Location: Along the cycle path and on the racecourse area at the A435 traffic lights (signs attached to columns). Description: Multiple temporary event notices remain attached to street light posts along the cycle path and around the racecourse by the A435 traffic lights. Those responsible were observed removing some notices after the event, however a number of signs have been left in place and remain in situ weeks later. These signs create visual clutter, detract from the street scene, and set a precedent for fly‑posting if not removed promptly. Request: Please arrange for the remaining signs to be removed and, if possible, follow up with the organisers to prevent recurrence. Duration: Approximately three weeks since the first event 29 March Safety impact: Could be hazardous, the location includes a cycle route and signalised junction, where unnecessary signage can distract road users.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Evesham Road, Bishop's Cleeve
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Chair Dumped, 21st April
item.description:
🗑️ We also noted fly‑tipping at the A435 with Southam Lane crossroads (a dumped chair)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southam Lane, Bishop's Cleeve
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road Arrow, 21st April
item.description:
📌 Confusing road marking where the carriageway merges – including an arrow that doesn’t match the direction of travel for cars travelling into Cheltenham🚗

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Evesham Road, Bishop's Cleeve
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Road marking white lines or studs

item.title: Chair at the Traffic light junction, 21st April
item.description:
On the Southam side of the carriageway by the cyclepath

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southam Lane, Bishop's Cleeve

item.title: Still causing severe delays on the main route to gloucester business park - should be disabled between 8am and 9am at the very least, 21st April
item.description:
Where all the traffic is queued up
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beamont Walk, Hucclecote, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish from bags left by commercial premicies, 20th April
item.description:
This evening seagulls seen damaging refuse bags awaiting collection in 3 areas of town centre, it will be an enormous clear up tomorrow.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Regent Street, Montpellier, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Several Signs have been hit by a vehicle and are now flat - including the WARNING CHEVRONS!!, 20th April
item.description:
Several Signs have been hit by a vehicle and are now flat - including the WARNING CHEVRONS!! Without the WARNING CHEVRONS there will be a serious accident, especially at night, with car drivers who don't know the road or who has rights of way. PLEASE PLEASE ESCALATE ASAP - THIS IS A MAJOR ISSUE
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): B4213, Deerhurst, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Sign structurally damaged/leaning more than 20 degrees/loose in ground (Non illuminating sign)

item.title: The bus stop sign is missing, it was knocked down years ago, 20th April
item.description:
The bus stop sign is missing from the entrance to coopers hill, opposite the entrance to Nut Hill
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Painswick Road, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Plastic tipped in a tree, 20th April
item.description:
It's wrapped around branches so needs a ladder and clippers opposite Normal Terrace

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 19, Saint Pauls Street South, Marle Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped bags, 20th April
item.description:
Lots of full black bags tipped opposite Normal Terrace

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 19, Saint Pauls Street South, Marle Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Several potholes not repaired, 20th April
item.description:
Old gloucester Road, several potholes not repaired, not even after closing the toad for a couple of hours. The road is atrocious to drive thru, cars are driving on the wrong side of the road to avoid the potholes. This has been like this for over 2 months…
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hayden Road, Hesters Way, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped item, 20th April
item.description:
40 inch TV outside the Hindu centre

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Milsom Street, Marle Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A number of pot holes inthe middle of the carriage way, getting bigger and deeper., 20th April
item.description:
Between Charlton kings nursing home and the Catholic church.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Moorend Road, Cheltenham

item.title: Potholes in road - endbof hall road, 20th April
item.description:
Lots of potholes need filling. Not marked with white spray, jut next to ones due for repair!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15, Hall Road, Leckhampton, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Traffic lights getting stuck on red one way multiple times a day. Causing people to drive very dangerously and get angry, beeping horns. Big problem that needs fixing, 20th April
item.description:
Shurdington Road road temporary traffic lights
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 92, Shurdington Road, Warden Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Light sensor is not detecting vehicles

item.title: Temporary traffic lights faulty, 20th April
item.description:
Temporary traffic lights on Leckhampton Road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 92, Shurdington Road, Warden Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Light sensor is not detecting vehicles

item.title: Lots of potholes you didn't fix, 20th April
item.description:
Im very worried about the people that said thay fixed it. Thay obviously have very serious problems with sight. As a safeguard issue the council need to look into this immediately as this is a safety issue. And a liability issue.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hayden Road, Hesters Way, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple pot holes end of hall road. Needs resurfacing, 20th April
item.description:
Some potholes have white marks around, but multiple potholes still need filling. Pointless waste of time doing half a job! Please resurfacing the end of hall road. It is causing damage to cars and bikes!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, Hall Road, Leckhampton, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
